Machine Learning Engineer
NeuralEdge AI — Remote

About NeuralEdge AI
NeuralEdge AI is an early-stage AI startup building recommendation and personalization systems for e-commerce. Our ML team trains and deploys models that power real-time product recommendations for millions of users. We move fast and care deeply about model quality and production reliability.

About the Role
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join our core ML team. You will own the full ML lifecycle: from data pipelines and feature engineering through model training, evaluation, and production deployment. You must be comfortable writing production Python code, running experiments, and operating MLOps infrastructure.

Responsibilities
- Design and implement data pipelines for ingesting, cleaning, and transforming training data
- Build feature engineering pipelines and maintain feature stores for model training and serving
- Train, evaluate, and tune deep learning models using PyTorch
- Track experiments and manage model versions using MLflow or Weights & Biases (W&B)
- Deploy models to production and monitor model performance and data drift
- Build and maintain ML training infrastructure using Docker and Kubernetes
- Collaborate with data engineers and product teams to define model inputs and business objectives
- Write clean, testable Python code for all ML components

Requirements
- 2+ years of ML engineering experience in a production environment
- Strong Python proficiency (primary language for all ML work)
- Hands-on experience with PyTorch for model training and inference
- Experience with MLOps tooling: MLflow, Weights & Biases, DVC, or equivalent experiment tracking and model registry tools
- Experience building data pipelines and feature engineering for ML models
- Familiarity with Kubernetes for deploying and scaling model serving infrastructure
- Experience with Docker for containerization of ML workloads
- Understanding of model evaluation metrics, cross-validation, and statistical testing
- Experience with experiment tracking and reproducibility best practices

Nice to Have
- Experience with recommendation systems or ranking models
- Familiarity with Apache Spark or similar distributed data processing
- Contributions to open-source ML projects
- Experience with real-time model serving (TorchServe, Triton Inference Server)

Compensation
$130,000–$165,000 base salary + equity + benefits
